parentQuestion: A 28-year-old woman presents to the ER after eating wild mushrooms. She has prof...

scaffolds.q: Muscarine is a toxin from certain mushrooms that mimics acetylcholine at muscarinic receptors.
scaffolds.explain: Muscarine binds to and activates muscarinic cholinergic receptors, producing parasympathetic effects. this is where the receptor got its name. The mushroom toxin taught us about this receptor subtype. Take a moment to appreciate this:the pharmacological history written in poisonings.

scaffolds.q: Parasympathetic activation increases secretions from glands and increases smooth muscle activity in the gut and bladder.
scaffolds.explain: The parasympathetic 'rest and digest' response cranks up secretory activity (saliva, tears, digestive juices) and GI motility. when these systems are overstimulated, you get excessive secretions and diarrhea. Embrace this:the parasympathetic overdrive.
